steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence already has an opening em dash after "black ash". The phrase "a durable and flexible [material]" is a non-essential appositive that must be closed with a matching em dash before the infinitive "to craft baskets." We need the choice that correctly closes the em-dash pair.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'material: to': A colon cannot close an em dash. This creates a punctuation mismatch. ✗
  • Choice B — 'material; to': A semicolon cannot close an em dash, and joining the infinitive phrase this way makes the sentence grammatically broken. ✗
  • Choice C — 'material—to': Supplies the closing em dash, properly enclosing "a durable and flexible material" between two em dashes, so "to craft baskets" follows naturally as the infinitive phrase. ✓
  • Choice D — 'material. To': A period ends the sentence mid-thought, leaving "To craft baskets..." as a fragment. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
